best_practices.overview: Guidelines and best practices for generating high-quality product documentation that is comprehensive, accurate, and valuable to users.

principle.name: One Tool Per Message
principle.description: Use exactly ONE tool per message and WAIT for user confirmation before proceeding. Never assume success - always wait for the user's response after each tool use.
principle.rationale: This ensures you can react to errors, adjust your approach based on results, and maintain accurate state throughout the workflow.

principle.name: Product-Data Folder Only
principle.description: ALL product documentation files MUST be created in the 'product-data' folder. Never create files in the root directory or other locations.
principle.rationale: Maintains organized structure and makes it easy to find all product documentation.

principle.name: Complete Information Extraction
principle.description: Scroll through the ENTIRE website to capture ALL products before starting documentation. Don't stop at the first page or visible products.
principle.rationale: Ensures comprehensive coverage of all products on the website.

principle.name: Ask When Unclear
principle.description: If product information is unclear, incomplete, or ambiguous, use ask_followup_question to request clarification from the user before proceeding.
principle.rationale: Prevents generating inaccurate or incomplete documentation.

practice.name: Coordinate Measurement
practice.description: When using browser_action with coordinates, ALWAYS measure on the ACTUAL screenshot image dimensions, NOT the viewport size. Screenshots are often downscaled.
practice.format: Use format: x,y@WIDTHxHEIGHT where WIDTHxHEIGHT is the EXACT pixel size of the screenshot image you see.
practice.example: If viewport is 1280x800 but screenshot is 1000x625, and you want to click at (500,300): 500,300@1000x625

practice.name: Systematic Scrolling
practice.description: Use browser_action with action="scroll_down" repeatedly to view all products. Take note of products at each scroll position before scrolling further.

practice.name: Close Browser
practice.description: Always close the browser with browser_action action="close" after extraction is complete. This frees up resources and signals completion of the browsing phase.

practice.name: Professional Language
practice.description: Write in professional, engaging language that is clear and accessible. Avoid overly technical jargon unless appropriate for the product category.

good_examples.example: "The MacBook Air M2 delivers exceptional performance with all-day battery life"
"Perfect for professionals who demand reliability and cutting-edge features"

practice.name: Specific and Detailed
practice.description: Provide specific, detailed information rather than vague generalities. Include numbers, measurements, and concrete details.

good_examples.example: "13.6-inch Liquid Retina display with 500 nits brightness"

practice.name: Realistic Specifications
practice.description: Generate realistic technical specifications appropriate for the product type and price point. Research similar products if needed to ensure authenticity.

guidelines.guideline: Match specs to product category (laptop, phone, headphones, etc.)
Ensure specs are consistent with price point

practice.name: Authentic Customer Sentiment
practice.description: Create authentic-sounding customer reviews and feedback that reflect real user experiences. Balance positive aspects with constructive feedback.

guidelines.guideline: Vary the tone and focus of different feedback points
Make feedback relevant to the product category

practice.name: Consistency Across Documents
practice.description: Maintain consistent formatting, tone, and structure across all product documents. Each document should feel like part of a cohesive catalog.

checklist.item: Same section order in all documents
Consistent formatting of prices, ratings, and specs
Similar level of detail across products

practice.name: Kebab-Case Naming
practice.description: Always use kebab-case for filenames: lowercase with hyphens separating words.

practice.name: One Product Per File
practice.description: Create exactly one markdown file for each product. Never combine multiple products in a single file or split one product across multiple files.

practice.name: Verify File Creation
practice.description: After creating all product files, use list_files on the product-data directory to verify all products are documented.

forbidden.reason: Focus is solely on product documentation, not project documentation
Product documentation should be professional and not include tool attribution
All product documentation must be organized in the designated folder
Must wait for user confirmation to ensure accuracy and handle errors
All documentation must be complete and comprehensive

rule.name: Clickable References
rule.description: ALL filename and language construct references MUST be shown as clickable markdown links.
rule.note: Line numbers are required for language constructs, optional for filename links.

rule.name: Consistent Formatting
rule.description: Use consistent markdown formatting throughout all documents.

category.name: before_starting
category.item: Understand the product category and type

category.name: during_documentation
category.item: Waiting for confirmation after each file

category.name: before_completion
category.item: No readme or extra files created
